name: Hejazi Arabic
notes: Hejazi Arabic (حجازي). Western Saudi Arabia: Mecca, Medina, Jeddah. DEFINING FEATURE: ق qaf → /ɡ/ (voiced velar stop) in inherited urban vocabulary — part of the early (8th c.) Hejazi chain shift /q/→/ɡ/→/dʒ/ (gīm), with /q/ retained only in learned/Classical borrowings; the older descriptions positing a /ʔ/ reflex here are a misattribution of the Levantine/Egyptian feature. ج jim → /dʒ/ (affricate). ث → /t/, ذ → /d/ (urban merger); interdentals preserved in Bedouin/rural speech. No k → /tʃ/ shift (unlike Gulf/Najdi). Cosmopolitan koine shaped by Hajj contact (Egyptian, Levantine, South Asian Arabic). Source: Hejazi Arabic phonology (Wikipedia); Watson (2002); Holes (2004).

sources.author: Watson, J.C.E.
sources.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic

sources.author: Holes, C.
sources.title: Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties
